# PDF Logo Fallback Design

**Date:** 2026-04-05  
**Status:** Approved

---

## Problem

When the configured `logoFile` does not exist on the server, the admin/client invoice PDF shows the `alt` text instead of a fallback icon.

**Root cause:** PDFs are generated by:
1. `renderToStaticMarkup(<AdminInvoice>)` — produces pure HTML, stripping all JS event handlers including `onError`
2. The HTML is sent to `/pdf/render` where Puppeteer renders it with `waitUntil: "networkidle0"`
3. Puppeteer waits for the image request to settle (404), then renders the `alt` text

The existing `onError` SVG fallback only works in the browser preview, not during PDF generation.

---

## Solution: Pre-fetch Logo as Base64

Before calling `renderToStaticMarkup`, the client fetches the logo URL. On success it converts to a base64 data URI; on failure it sets `logoSrc = null`. This is passed as a prop to the invoice component.

**Why base64 embed is deployment-safe:**
- Puppeteer no longer needs to resolve any external URL during PDF rendering
- Works on VPS, Railway, Render, or any environment where the Express server runs
- `fetch(logoURL)` runs in the browser (not Puppeteer), so CORS and auth behave normally
- If `VITE_API_BASE` is correctly set for the deployment, the fetch will always reach the right server

---

## Architecture

### `History.jsx` — `handleDownload`

Add logo pre-fetch before `renderToStaticMarkup`:

```js
const logoURL = `${import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE}/public/${companyConfig.logoFile}`;
let logoSrc = null;
try {
  const logoRes = await fetch(logoURL);
  if (logoRes.ok) {
    const blob = await logoRes.blob();
    logoSrc = await new Promise((resolve) => {
      const reader = new FileReader();
      reader.onload = () => resolve(reader.result);
      reader.readAsDataURL(blob);
    });
  }
} catch {
  // Logo missing or unreachable — SVG fallback will be used
}
const html = renderToStaticMarkup(<Component invoice={invoice} logoSrc={logoSrc} />);
```

### `AdminInvoice.jsx` + `ClientInvoice.jsx` — `logoSrc` prop

Accept an optional `logoSrc` prop. Three cases:

| `logoSrc` value | Source | Renders |
|---|---|---|
| `string` (base64 data URI) | PDF download, logo found | `<img src={logoSrc}>` — Puppeteer uses inline data, no network needed |
| `null` | PDF download, logo missing | Inline house SVG — no `<img>` tag at all |
| `undefined` (not passed) | Browser preview | Existing: URL + `onError` handler (no change) |

The fallback SVG (house icon, 36×36, `stroke='#94a3b8'`) is already defined in the existing `onError` handlers — it will be extracted as a shared inline element.

---

## Deployment Notes

### VPS (Ubuntu/Debian)
- Express + Puppeteer run as a persistent process — fully supported
- Puppeteer requires system Chromium dependencies:
  ```
  apt-get install -y ca-certificates fonts-liberation libappindicator3-1 \
    libasound2 libatk-bridge2.0-0 libatk1.0-0 libc6 libcairo2 libcups2 \
    libdbus-1-3 libexpat1 libfontconfig1 libgbm1 libgcc1 libglib2.0-0 \
    libgtk-3-0 libnspr4 libnss3 libpango-1.0-0 libpangocairo-1.0-0 \
    libstdc++6 libx11-6 libx11-xcb1 libxcb1 libxcomposite1 libxcursor1 \
    libxdamage1 libxext6 libxfixes3 libxi6 libxrandr2 libxrender1 \
    libxss1 libxtst6 lsb-release wget xdg-utils
  ```
- Logo file lives at `server/public/<logoFile>` — upload it during deployment or via `scp`/`rsync`
- Set `VITE_API_BASE` in the client build env to the deployed API URL (e.g. `https://api.yourdomain.com`)

### Railway / Render (PaaS with persistent server)
- Same as VPS — Express + Puppeteer supported
- Puppeteer dependencies: add `puppeteer` to `package.json` (already present); these platforms install Chromium automatically via the `puppeteer` postinstall script
- Logo file: either commit a default logo to `server/public/` or upload via the platform's persistent disk feature
- Set `VITE_API_BASE` as an environment variable in the platform dashboard

### Vercel (frontend only)
- **Vercel cannot run Express + Puppeteer** — serverless functions have no persistent process, no Chrome binary, and a 50 MB size limit
- Recommended split: deploy **frontend** to Vercel, deploy **backend** (Express + Puppeteer) to Railway/Render/VPS
- Set `VITE_API_BASE` in Vercel's environment variables to point to the separately hosted backend URL

### Environment variable checklist for any deployment
| Variable | Where set | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_API_BASE` | Client build env | `https://api.yourdomain.com` |
| `MONGODB_URI` | Server env | `mongodb+srv://...` |
| `JWT_SECRET` | Server env | (random string) |

---

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `client/src/pages/History.jsx` | Pre-fetch logo, pass `logoSrc` prop |
| `client/src/components/AdminInvoice.jsx` | Accept `logoSrc` prop, conditional render |
| `client/src/components/ClientInvoice.jsx` | Accept `logoSrc` prop, conditional render |

`Compare.jsx` uses `CompareInvoices` for its PDF — same pattern applies and will be fixed identically.

| `client/src/pages/Compare.jsx` | Pre-fetch logo, pass `logoSrc` prop |
| `client/src/components/CompareInvoices.jsx` | Accept `logoSrc` prop, conditional render |

---

## Out of Scope

- `ClientInvoiceTemp.jsx` — not used for PDF download, browser-only component; existing `onError` is sufficient
- Changing the PDF engine (Puppeteer stays)
- Logo upload UI — logo is still placed manually in `server/public/`
